At fifteen, Maya was a quiet girl in the classroom. She never talks and does not say her thoughts. She thought she was too small but wanted to help. When she learned about climate change, everything changed for her. Maya was eight when her teacher showed films about nature. The films show plastic in the ocean and it is bad. They showed polar bears and they had no food. They showed forests that are burning. Maya cried when she watched all the movies. Her classmates felt sad when they watched the films. When the films stopped, her classmates thought about other things. Maya cannot do that. The pictures stay in her head and make her think. Maya thinks about climate change and feels worried. She wondered if she has a future. She kept her worries to herself because she did not talk much. That was not good for your health. Maya feels very sad and becomes depressed. She stopped going to school because she felt tired. When Maya was home, her parents cared for her. They talked because they had nothing else to do. Maya told them what she worries about. She told them about the problem with the climate. She felt good when she shared her feelings. Her parents say everything will be all right for her. That did not help, but it was expected. It was good to talk and share feelings. Maya talked a lot about climate change. She showed her parents pictures and charts. She showed them movies and articles about the topic. After some time, her parents began to listen. Maya realized she can make a difference when she tries. Maya feels better when she thinks it is a waste of time. I can do a lot of good with my life. I still try to do that now. Maya found out she could persuade people easily. Her mother stopped flying and changed her job. Her father is a vegetarian now. Over the years, her father had no more arguments. Maya showed him many documentaries and he liked them. They read books together and enjoy their time. Before that, her father did not know anything at all. He thought the climate problem was fixed. Maya changed her family and made them happy. Now she changes other people too. One day in August, Maya painted a sign on wood. She wrote facts that she thought everyone should know. She took her bike and went to the parliament building. She sat alone and felt very lonely. On the first day, she sat alone from morning until afternoon. It was a normal day at school. On the second day, people join her and have fun. There were people there all the time after that. Maya kept her promise and struck every day. She agreed to speak in front of many people. Her parents do not want to help her. Maya was very quiet, so doctors said she had selective mutism. Her parents tried to stop her, but she did not listen. Maya is strong and she wants to succeed. She said she does not change her mind when she is passionate. Maya gave the speech in perfect English, but her family worried. She invites the crowd to film her with their phones. She asked them to share the message on social media. Her father cried because he felt proud. Maya is quiet and does not want to change. That was just who she was. If she was like others, she would try to start a group. But she cannot do that. She is not good with people and feels lonely. So she did it by herself instead. Maya is active on climate and she is no longer lonely. She is not silent anymore. She is not so sad now. She was busy and tried to make a difference. She enjoys herself and feels happy. Maya said it will be very big around the world. Hundreds of thousands of children strike from school for a better future. They say we will not accept this anymore. I think we are just seeing the start now. I think change is coming soon and it feels good. People stand up for their future and want change. Maya went back to being a teenager again. She said she looks forward to it. I want to see all the pictures the next day. It will be fun and exciting for everyone. Maya was not the only young person who helped others. Emma was eleven years old and felt frustrated. Every book in her class has white boys and their dogs. Emma reads five books in one year. There are no characters who look like her. Emma saw that this pattern happened often. She feels frustrated when things do not go well. Emma liked the stories and her teacher, but she wanted more. She wanted to see other people and their stories. Emma has three choices. Option one: focus on herself and get more books. Option two: find authors and ask them to write more books like her. Emma chose option three and changed the system. Emma started a campaign to collect and give one thousand books. She named it the Thousand Books campaign. Emma reached her goal and felt very happy. She collected many books from different countries and loved them. The community needed the books, so they donated them. Emma wrote a book to help kids be activists. The hardest part of the campaign was learning about entitlement. When Emma helps people, she feels like a charity. She gives the books to you and wants praise for Emma. But that was not something she wanted. Emma stays calm and does not fly away. She does not want her friends to see her differently. Sometimes she does not tell her friends what she does. She did not tell them a lie. She said she is going to work and needs to help her mom. Emma has a message for kids and wants to share it. Emma said she wants to tell the girls they can help. You should tell the person to stop that idea. You can help and make a difference in the world. If you believe in your words, your dreams can come true. Emma said that frustration is energy. It can help make a new and useful idea. To be brave, you must focus on finding your passions. You do not have to wait to be great. At eight, Sofia saw her mother put ointment on her sister. Her sister has strange rashes on her body. Sofia and her family do not know the cause. They thought it was the soap or something else. They found out it came from the water. The water from their taps has lead and other bad things. When Sofia learned about the water problem, she wrote to the President. She asked him to come and see her city. My mom said you are busy with important things, Sofia wrote. Many people come on these buses and fill the space. A meeting from you or your wife would lift people's spirits. Everyone is surprised because the President writes back. You are right that Presidents are busy, he said. In our country, being a citizen is the most important title. Letters from kids like you make me hopeful for the future. The President came to Sofia's city and talked to people. Sofia is a voice for clean water and helps many people. She helps people with bad water in many places. One day, a company sent Sofia's family a water filter. The family gave it to an old neighbor who needed it. Sofia now helps people in many places, not just her city. She helps cities where water is bad and people need support. Sofia tells her sister and aunt she does not want to talk. She wants to help set up the toy drive. Sofia said one girl can change the world. It is big and small at the same time. Maya, Emma, and Sofia show that young people can change things. Maya thought she was small and felt sad. Emma feels frustrated about what she sees. Sofia saw her family and felt sad. They all took action and worked together. They all helped and made a difference. They all showed that you can be great now. You can begin now and do it well. Your voice is important, even when you are a child. One person can start a big change and help many others. Frustration can help us change and grow better. I thought I could not help because I was too small. But I was wrong about what I thought.
